learning_objectives.text: Define emerging and re-emerging infectious diseases and analyze the ecological, social, and technological drivers of disease emergence
Apply One Health principles to evaluate zoonotic disease spillover events and surveillance strategies
Evaluate the effectiveness of global health security architectures including IHR, GHSA, and pandemic preparedness systems
Design evidence-based antimicrobial resistance surveillance and stewardship programs for different healthcare settings
Analyze the impact of climate change on infectious disease emergence patterns and develop adaptation strategies
Evaluate risk communication strategies for health emergencies and assess infodemic management approaches

key_topics.topic: Definitions and Drivers of Disease Emergence
subtopics: Ecological factors: deforestation, biodiversity loss, land use change
Social factors: urbanization, population density, travel patterns
Technological factors: healthcare practices, agricultural intensification
Economic factors: poverty, inequality, food systems
Temperature and precipitation effects on disease vectors
Extreme weather events and outbreak risks

case_studies.title: MERS-CoV Emergence and Response in the Arabian Peninsula
key_lessons: Zoonotic spillover from camels to humans
Risk communication challenges during Hajj pilgrimage

case_studies.title: Measles Resurgence in Europe: Vaccine Hesitancy and Coverage Gaps
key_lessons: Factors contributing to declining vaccination coverage
Role of vaccine hesitancy and misinformation
Emergency response and outbreak control measures

case_studies.title: Carbapenem-Resistant Enterobacteriaceae Response in India
key_lessons: Antimicrobial stewardship in diverse healthcare settings

case_studies.title: Post-Ebola Health Security Strengthening in West Africa
